title: component signature
text: component signature Description of the remote interface of a component, which is used to access it at runtime. Legacy Notes: When it compiles a Uniface component, Uniface automatically generates the signature. The description for a component that is used at run time to access it. The component signature consists of an interface signature plus one or more implementation signatures. The interface signature represents Uniface's view of the component; the implementation signature represents the external view. The signature is created automatically when a Uniface component is compiled. The signature for a non-Uniface component must be created with the Define Signature form. In either case, when the (component) signature is compiled, a signature descriptor, stored in ULANA, is created. Related Links signature Description of the remote interface of a component or a modeled entity operation, which is used to access it at runtime. Signatures for Uniface components and modeled entity operations are automatically generated, but signatures for non-Uniface components that Uniface can call at runtime, must be explicitly defined.
